---
title: "Section 9: Ethics"
author: "Matt Higham"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Class Exercises 

Class Exercise 1. The purpose of doing this activity is to see that, a survye that does not require you to give your name can still potentially identify you if you give enough identifying information. For example, providing your sex, reason, and year in a survey for a class could potentially identify you.

Class Exercise 2. (c) would be most okay: there is no sensitive information being shared. (a) would probably be fine if you assume that people do not know others' Canvas patterns.

both B and d, with a small enough class, yo9u could potentially identify someone's gpa with the right combination. so these would be bad.


Your Turn

Your Turn 1. Hypothesis generation is more focused on creating many visualizations or tables with a single set of data (like we do in this class, STAT 234). Hypothesis confirmation is more about setting up and then answering a single question of interest (like a lot of what you did in STAT 113).


Your Turn 2. For generation/exploration, as many times as you want! For confirmation, it's just once.

Your Turn 3.

a. Confirmation (one question of interest to answer, using each day a single time).

b. Generation/Exploration (there are many questions that this single data set is trying to answer).

c. ???? the last part of the prompt makes more hypothesis generation.
